summary.headline: Modeled 500 units across 3 destination bucket(s); multi-warehouse total $3311.30 vs best single-warehouse path $5134.01 (delta multi minus single $-1822.71).

options.title: Multi-warehouse: best DC per destination
options.tradeoffs: Parcel from whichever recommended DC is cheapest for each destination bucket. No modeled inbound linehaul between your sites on this path.

options.title: Single-warehouse: linehaul + parcel via 07001 (NJ)
options.tradeoffs: Inbound linehaul into one receive DC, then parcel to customers. Can win when linehaul + outbound beats multi-warehouse parcel in the mocks.

options.title: Single-warehouse: linehaul + parcel via 33101 (FL)
options.tradeoffs: Inbound linehaul into one receive DC, then parcel to customers. Can win when linehaul + outbound beats multi-warehouse parcel in the mocks.

methodology.strategy_multi_warehouse: Parcel from each **origin** to each destination bucket; cheapest origin wins per bucket (multi-warehouse outbound).
methodology.strategy_single_warehouse: Mock linehaul to receive DC, then parcel from **receive node ZIP** to customers — single-warehouse outbound from that DC (final mile not from the original origin).
methodology.shippo_mock_note: When parcel legs use integrated pricing, SHIPPO_MOCK_MODE shapes RateShoppingService quotes; linehaul is still mock.
methodology.fbm_warehouse_model: FBM: multi-warehouse totals include pick/pack + batch packaging/order fees by ship-from DC; single-warehouse totals include inbound receive (mock ASN/pallet) + outbound pick/pack at receive node (Unie-style rate_card mocks). FBA: transport-only scenario for comparison — see fba_comparative_guidance.

multi_warehouse.description: Multi-warehouse: ship from the cheapest recommended DC per destination bucket (parcel-only from origins; no modeled inbound linehaul between your DCs).

single_warehouse.description: Single-warehouse: modeled inbound linehaul to one receive DC, then parcel to destinations (final mile priced from the receive node, not the original origin).

vocabulary.recommendation_codes_note: recommendation linehaul_then_parcel means prefer single-warehouse path; noop means no change vs threshold — see recommended_network_path and delta_multi_warehouse_minus_single_warehouse_usd.

network_fulfillment_economics.savings_pct_note: 100 * (single_warehouse - multi_warehouse) / single_warehouse on all-in fulfillment $/unit. Positive => multi is cheaper; negative => multi costs more.
100 * (single_warehouse - multi_warehouse) / single_warehouse on all-in fulfillment $/unit. Positive => multi is cheaper; negative => multi costs more.

item_intelligence_pipeline.name: placement_mock_rate_grids
item_intelligence_pipeline.detail: 48 contiguous-state hub ZIPs mock parcel quotes per warehouse (O/D zones); optional share merge.

item_intelligence_pipeline.name: allocation
item_intelligence_pipeline.detail: allocate_skus from demand + warehouse target_share_pct (possibly from mock grid).

item_intelligence_pipeline.name: landed_cost_economics
item_intelligence_pipeline.detail: Per-SKU fully loaded $/unit (parcel mock, transfer, label, receiving, handling, storage) + negotiation levers.

item_intelligence_pipeline.name: fulfillment_network_comparison
item_intelligence_pipeline.detail: Single-hub vs allocated $/unit with intelligence (verdict, rankings, drivers, actions).

item_intelligence_pipeline.name: item_intelligence_synthesis
item_intelligence_pipeline.detail: Unified per-SKU intelligence: fulfillment + economics + placement + negotiation priorities.

network_placement_adjustment.rationale: At least one hub→node lane moves fewer than 100 units/month; planning cover was extended to 3 month(s) of flow so a replenishment batch can meet the minimum move size.

buy_box_market_summary.note: Buy-box window counts come from buyBoxSellerIdHistory; offer snapshot is row concentration in one Keepa fetch.

similar_seller_buybox_metrics.cohort_note: no client listing signals — all followers treated as peer cohort
similar_seller_buybox_metrics.note: Win % is time-on-buy-box in Keepa window, not unit sales share.

procurement_suggestion.prompt_for_buyer: Planning velocity ~322 units/mo (~10.7407/day); for ~30d cover, target ~322 units on hand (monthly band 242–429).

possible_upgrades.impact: Align planning to this seller's buy-box share or offer-row share.

placement_mock_rate_grids.excluded_states_note: Alaska and Hawaii are excluded; 48 contiguous states only.

parcel_assumptions.multi_sku_parcel_note: Share merge uses catalog median weight; per-SKU economics and fulfillment re-mean mock parcel from warehouse_grids when SKU weight differs by >0.01 lb.

demand_weighting.refresh_policy: Annual or on deploy; replace table in us_state_demand_share.py or future config-driven loader.
demand_weighting.seasonality_note: Static shares — no within-year seasonal adjustment in-model.
demand_weighting.note: Planning prior; override with label-derived state mix when available.

placement_mock_rate_grids.note: Same 48 contiguous-state hub ZIPs for every warehouse (one hot metro per state; no AK/HI). Each DC is rate-shopped to all hubs including its own state's hub. carrier_zone_origin_to_destination is mock O/D zone for the winning carrier. demand_weighted_expected_mock_parcel_usd_network uses state demand weights and state_primary_assignment (min_mock_parcel or distance_tie_band).

network_placement_adjustment.rationale: At least one hub→node lane moves fewer than 100 units/month; planning cover was extended to 3 month(s) of flow so a replenishment batch can meet the minimum move size.

allocation.note: V1 proportional allocator; use CUOPT_NIM_URL path for VRP-grade optimization.

cohort.note: At equilibrium, monthly outflow from the stocked position should match monthly demand; peak ≈ demand × cover_months so peak/cover_months ≈ demand.

outbound_customer_shipment.note: Fully loaded includes exactly one outbound customer-ship cost: observed label buy rate when avg_label_amount_usd is present, otherwise the mock parcel benchmark. mock_outbound_parcel_usd_per_unit is always the benchmark; label_usd_per_unit is 0 when the mock is used for the total.

inter_warehouse_positioning.note: Lane $/lb × weight × monthly hub→node flow; divide by demand for per-unit sold.

cohort.note: At equilibrium, monthly outflow from the stocked position should match monthly demand; peak ≈ demand × cover_months so peak/cover_months ≈ demand.

per_sku.notes: Outbound ship: one line in fully loaded — label buy rate from SKU history when present, else mock parcel (demand-weighted network when grid exposes it). mock_outbound_parcel_usd_per_unit is always the benchmark; label_usd_per_unit is non-zero only when avg_label_amount_usd is set. Transfer = lane $/lb × weight × monthly hub→node flow ÷ demand. Storage = network-blend $/unit-in-stock/month × time-weighted avg on-hand ÷ monthly demand.

negotiation_suggestions.talk_track: Receiving is modeled at ~$0.159/unit (network blend). A 5% concession saves ~$0.008/unit on every inbound unit — ask for tiered receiving or carton flat rates that amortize over your batch sizes.
Receiving is modeled at ~$0.159/unit (network blend). A 10% concession saves ~$0.016/unit on every inbound unit — ask for tiered receiving or carton flat rates that amortize over your batch sizes.

negotiation_suggestions.current_assumption_note: Lane cost × weight × monthly transfer flow ÷ demand.
negotiation_suggestions.talk_track: Inter-DC linehaul is a direct multiplier on weight × flow. Negotiate MWB/min charge, backhaul lanes, or multi-stop milk runs to cut effective $/lb.

negotiation_suggestions.scenario: If placement shifts volume to 5% cheaper mean-zone warehouses
negotiation_suggestions.talk_track: Mock grid assumes each SKU ships from its allocated DC to representative US hubs. Better share allocation + carrier incentives on those lanes moves blended parcel $/unit.
Where label history exists, compare to benchmark rates; where not, parcel mock is a ceiling. Negotiate carrier incentives on outbound from each node.

fulfillment_network_comparison.description: Fully loaded $/unit includes exactly one outbound customer-ship cost: observed label buy rate when avg_label_amount_usd is set, otherwise the mock parcel benchmark (demand-weighted network expectation when the grid exposes it, scaled for SKU weight). mock_outbound_parcel_usd_per_unit remains the benchmark; label_usd_per_unit is zero when mock carries the total. Transfer $/unit is zero for single-hub columns. Multi-node uses share split + hub→node lane model.

coverage_vs_inventory_reconciliation.basis: geographic_routing_share_demand_weighted: sum of state demand weights where this DC is primary under state_primary_assignment (min_mock_parcel or distance_tie_band). inventory_allocation_share: normalized allocation target_share.
coverage_vs_inventory_reconciliation.note: Mismatches imply stock placement / inbound splits / transfers may not match modeled last-mile routing. See placement_mock_rate_grids.state_shipping_coverage for state→DC detail.

adjustable_model_inputs.note: Structured levers that move modeled $/unit; SLA, dangerous goods, tax nexus, and carrier capacity constraints are out of model. Cross-check fulfillment_network_comparison.drivers and landed_cost_economics.negotiation_suggestions.

knobs.effect_higher: Raises inter-warehouse transfer $/unit (hub→spoke flows).
Changes mock parcel zones and which DC is primary per state.
Shifts fee blends and inventory placement; does not change demand-weighted geography.
More nodes only when monthly demand clears MOQ-style floors.
Raises mock parcel $ through DIM/weight in grid quotes.
Widens shared-state routing between DCs (distance assignment mode).
Switches state→DC primary between min_mock_parcel and distance_tie_band.
Direct add to fully loaded $/unit (network blend by allocation share).
Becomes the sole outbound ship $/unit in fully loaded when set (mock stays as benchmark only).
Shifts demand_weighted_expected_mock_parcel_usd_network toward high-share states.
Blends state weights toward observed label mix (see demand_weighting on grid).
Raises peak/avg on-hand → storage $/unit sold.

single_hub_scenarios.interpretation: Allocated network costs more per unit than this single-hub option
Allocated network costs more per unit than this single-hub option
Allocated network costs more per unit than this single-hub option
Allocated network costs more per unit than this single-hub option

side_by_side_cost_comparison.basis: USD per unit sold at stated monthly demand; positive delta = multi-node costs more than that single-hub column.

columns.single_hub_reference: 100% of demand ships from one DC; inter-DC transfer $/unit = 0

line_items_usd_per_unit.line_item_label: Outbound parcel mock (benchmark to 48 US hub ZIPs; not added to total if label buy rate is used)
Observed label buy rate (0 = mock parcel carries outbound in fully loaded total)

per_sku.narrative: Fully loaded model: allocated network is ~$0.1162/unit more expensive than fulfilling everything from NJ alone in this mock — transfer and blended parcel net negative vs that single origin.

cohort.note: At equilibrium, monthly outflow from the stocked position should match monthly demand; peak ≈ demand × cover_months so peak/cover_months ≈ demand.

intelligence.headline: SKU DEMO-B0DT44TSM2: Fulfilling 100% from NJ is ~$0.1163/unit cheaper than the current split+transfer model.

recommended_moves_to_match_or_beat_singl: Closing the $0.1163/unit gap: modeled inter-DC linehaul loads ~$0.1163/unit on each sold unit — cut effective hub→spoke $/lb by ~100.0% **if** linehaul scales linearly, or eliminate the leg via supplier/PO drops to the spoke DC.
Stack levers: (1) linehaul/MWB (2) inbound vendor splits to spoke (3) outbound rate shop per node — model is linear on $/lb transfer.

illustrative_share_nudge_parcel_effect.description: Illustrative: move 5.0 share points from higher-mean parcel node CA toward lower-mean FL (re-run allocation + ops plan before executing).

recommended_actions: Closing the $0.1163/unit gap: modeled inter-DC linehaul loads ~$0.1163/unit on each sold unit — cut effective hub→spoke $/lb by ~100.0% **if** linehaul scales linearly, or eliminate the leg via supplier/PO drops to the spoke DC.
Stack levers: (1) linehaul/MWB (2) inbound vendor splits to spoke (3) outbound rate shop per node — model is linear on $/lb transfer.
Inter-DC positioning costs ~1.06% of fully loaded $/unit; validate whether split inventory is strategically required before absorbing that drag.
If you must stay multi-node, prioritize cutting effective $/lb on hub→spoke lanes or increasing transfer batch sizes so linehaul amortizes better.
caveats: Mock parcel uses demand-weighted national expectation when the grid includes it; otherwise unweighted mean to 48 hubs.
Real lane mix, contracts, and DIM weight will differ from mocks.
Label $ uses SKU history when present, else parcel proxy — tighten with carrier data.
coverage_vs_inventory_reconciliation compares routing share to allocation share; they need not match operationally.

adjustable_model_inputs.note: Structured levers that move modeled $/unit; SLA, dangerous goods, tax nexus, and carrier capacity constraints are out of model. Cross-check fulfillment_network_comparison.drivers and landed_cost_economics.negotiation_suggestions.

recommended_actions: Closing the $0.1163/unit gap: modeled inter-DC linehaul loads ~$0.1163/unit on each sold unit — cut effective hub→spoke $/lb by ~100.0% **if** linehaul scales linearly, or eliminate the leg via supplier/PO drops to the spoke DC.
Stack levers: (1) linehaul/MWB (2) inbound vendor splits to spoke (3) outbound rate shop per node — model is linear on $/lb transfer.
Inter-DC positioning costs ~1.06% of fully loaded $/unit; validate whether split inventory is strategically required before absorbing that drag.
If you must stay multi-node, prioritize cutting effective $/lb on hub→spoke lanes or increasing transfer batch sizes so linehaul amortizes better.
caveats: Mock parcel uses demand-weighted national expectation when the grid includes it; otherwise unweighted mean to 48 hubs.
Real lane mix, contracts, and DIM weight will differ from mocks.
Label $ uses SKU history when present, else parcel proxy — tighten with carrier data.
coverage_vs_inventory_reconciliation compares routing share to allocation share; they need not match operationally.

cohort.note: At equilibrium, monthly outflow from the stocked position should match monthly demand; peak ≈ demand × cover_months so peak/cover_months ≈ demand.

outbound_customer_shipment.note: Fully loaded includes exactly one outbound customer-ship cost: observed label buy rate when avg_label_amount_usd is present, otherwise the mock parcel benchmark. mock_outbound_parcel_usd_per_unit is always the benchmark; label_usd_per_unit is 0 when the mock is used for the total.

inter_warehouse_positioning.note: Lane $/lb × weight × monthly hub→node flow; divide by demand for per-unit sold.

cohort.note: At equilibrium, monthly outflow from the stocked position should match monthly demand; peak ≈ demand × cover_months so peak/cover_months ≈ demand.

negotiation_priorities: inbound_receiving_fee_per_unit: If warehouses reduce receiving by 5% → ~$0.0079/unit illustrative savings in model.
inbound_receiving_fee_per_unit: If warehouses reduce receiving by 10% → ~$0.0159/unit illustrative savings in model.
inter_warehouse_transport_cost_per_lb: If carrier reduces hub-to-node $/lb by 10% → ~$0.0116/unit illustrative savings in model.

placement.note: Placement cover adjusted to ~90 days (3 mo. batches) to meet min inter-DC move — target ~967 units network cover.

us_state_demand_forecast.refresh_policy: Annual or on deploy; replace table in us_state_demand_share.py or future config-driven loader.
us_state_demand_forecast.seasonality_note: Static shares — no within-year seasonal adjustment in-model.
us_state_demand_forecast.note: Planning prior; override with label-derived state mix when available.

multi_warehouse.path: direct_multi_origin (parcel from each origin; cheapest per destination)
multi_warehouse.inbound_receiving_note: This path does not add inbound ASN/receive at origin - only outbound pick fees by ship-from DC + one batch of order/packaging fees for the scenario qty.

single_warehouse.path: consolidated (mock linehaul to receive DC + parcel from receive ZIP)

scenario_fulfillment_breakdown.storage_and_long_term_note: Ongoing storage rent is not included in network scenario all-in totals. Item intelligence landed_cost_economics adds amortized storage (and flat receive/handling from profiles) for per-SKU planning.

item_intelligence_fulfillment_components.note: Per-SKU model: outbound ship uses historical label $/unit when available else mock parcel benchmark; plus inter-DC transfer, inbound receiving, outbound handling, and storage amortized over monthly demand.
